exploration_log.aha_moment: The class doc comment at line 82-85 says 'the purpose of wrapping here is to allow closing a reader to *also* close the command'. The Dispose(bool) already does this at lines 134-135, but Close() only closes the reader. The fix is to match Dispose(bool) behavior in Close().
exploration_log.hindsight: Should also check if CloseAsync() needs similar treatment - it delegates to _reader.CloseAsync() without disposing _cmd.

alternatives: Make Close() call Dispose() which calls Dispose(bool disposing: true)
Duplicate the _cmd disposal logic directly in Close()
key_decisions.decision: Duplicate the disposal logic in Close() matching exactly what Dispose(bool) does
key_decisions.reasoning: The simplest, most direct fix that matches the documented purpose. Calling Dispose() from Close() could cause double-dispose issues if the caller also disposes later.

search_sequence.reasoning: Task description named this class and file
search_sequence.result: Found Close() at line 110 only closing _reader without _cmd; Dispose(bool) at lines 128-136 does dispose both

solve_notes: The `DbWrappedReader.Close()` method only called `_reader.Close()`, leaving `_cmd` undisposed. The fix adds `_cmd?.Dispose()` and nulls `_cmd` in `Close()`, matching what `Dispose(bool)` already did and fulfilling the documented purpose ('allowing closing a reader to also close the command').
task_text:
Fix DbWrappedReader.Close not disposing the underlying command

`DbWrappedReader` in `WrappedReader.cs` is documented as allowing
"closing a reader to also close the command", but its `Close()` method
only calls `_reader.Close()` without disposing `_cmd`. The `Dispose(bool)`
overload correctly disposes both, but `Close()` is the path taken when
query code finishes reading rows. Fix `WrappedReader.cs` so `Close()`
also disposes `_cmd`, matching the documented purpose and the behavior
of `Dispose(bool)`.

thrash_preventing_defs.name: Dispose
thrash_preventing_defs.reason: read: an AI agent must read Dispose(bool) to see the existing _cmd?.Dispose() pattern and replicate it exactly in Close()

tier_difference_reasoning: The minimum set is just `Close` (the edited method). `Dispose(bool)` is added to thrash_preventing because an AI agent needs to see the existing disposal pattern before implementing Close(), to ensure it replicates exactly the same logic (_cmd?.Dispose() then _cmd = null!) rather than inventing something different.
